summary: Session 3 (reminders, recurrence, no-show/deposit) tested. Backend 11/11 new + 27 regression = 38/38 pytest PASS. Frontend: dashboard widget-reminders + sent badge + sms: link + click-to-mark-sent OK; recurrence-section + recur-weeks-4/5/6 + input + create-btn OK; recurrence hidden on cancelled RDV OK; client-risk-banner shown for Sophie (Acompte requis 10€ demandés) + hidden for safe/plain client OK; client-noshow-card + 6 stat cards + deposit toggle checked + deposit note '10€ demandés' + save-client-btn OK; reminder-section + template textarea + save-reminder-btn OK; backup-section + next-visit-admin regression OK. CRITICAL frontend bug found and fixed inline (2 missing useState declarations in AppointmentForm.jsx).

minor.issue: weeks=0 in payload is coerced to default 5 (via `payload.get('weeks') or 5`) instead of being clamped to 1. Fix: use `payload.get('weeks', 5) if payload.get('weeks') is not None else 5` or handle 0 explicitly before the clamp.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing useState declarations for `clientRisk`/`setClientRisk` and `recurWeeks`/`setRecurWeeks` — Uncaught ReferenceError: setClientRisk is not defined, then recurWeeks is not defined. The entire /rdv/* page (edit and create) crashed to the runtime error overlay, no testids rendered. Fixed inline by adding both useState hooks so remaining tests could proceed.

action_items: Confirm keep of the two useState fixes in /app/frontend/src/pages/AppointmentForm.jsx (added: `const [clientRisk, setClientRisk] = useState(null);` after clientLoyalty; `const [recurWeeks, setRecurWeeks] = useState(5);` after loadingSlots).
Optional (minor): fix weeks=0 fallback in POST /appointments/{rid}/schedule-next so it clamps to 1 (currently returns +5w via truthy-fallback).

Optional cosmetic: reminder SMS pre-filled message can render `()` empty parentheses when services list on the appointment is empty for a fresh RDV — cosmetic only; happens because {services} template resolves to empty string.
critical_code_review_comments: AppointmentForm.jsx shipped without the useState hooks that its own useEffect and JSX rely on — indicates the recurrence + risk banner features were never actually opened during dev. Recommend a smoke render test.

PUT /api/clients/{cid} accepts arbitrary Dict[str, Any] and $set's it verbatim — no allow-list, so any key (including internal fields like access_token or lat/lng) could be overwritten by clients. Consider a ClientUpdate Pydantic model.
Recurrence: schedule-next re-uses source RDV `client_name` snapshot instead of re-reading the client doc — will silently drift if client is renamed between the two RDVs.
reminders/tomorrow query loads all scheduled appointments (up to 5000) then filters in Python; consider a Mongo range query on `date` for scale.

success_rate.frontend: 100% after AppointmentForm hotfix (was 0% on /rdv/* pages before)

test_credentials: PIN 123456; Sophie cli_e998044705; existing tomorrow RDV rdv_a67c9cf136.
seed_data_creation: Temporary RDVs created + deleted (test_new_tomorrow_rdv_sent_false_then_marked + 3 recurrence RDVs cleaned by test_zz_cleanup; UI test created rdv_21dcca3f6b and deleted it).
context_for_next_testing_agent: AppointmentForm.jsx was missing two useState hooks (clientRisk, recurWeeks). I added them so the page renders. If main agent restructures state, keep these two hooks. Backend clamp bug for weeks=0 is documented but unfixed (test uses weeks=-1 to hit lower bound).
rca of the issue: AppointmentForm.jsx used setClientRisk() inside a useEffect (l.66/74) and recurWeeks inside the Recurrence JSX block (l.442-465) but the corresponding useState declarations were absent, causing an uncaught ReferenceError that crashed the entire form as soon as /rdv/:id or /rdv/nouveau was opened. Repro before fix: navigate to /rdv/rdv_a67c9cf136 → runtime error overlay 'setClientRisk is not defined'. Mitigation: added `const [clientRisk, setClientRisk] = useState(null);` (after `clientLoyalty`) and `const [recurWeeks, setRecurWeeks] = useState(5);` (after `loadingSlots`). After fix, all recurrence + client-risk-banner flows verified working end-to-end.
